rulings.Case Title: PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. 44 NORMANDIE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 20, 2012, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT AL'S COMPLETE WELDING SERVICE, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied in its entirety. rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER RYS VS. JOSEPH BRADFORD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 20, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER RYS' Motion For Reconsideration. Granted. The court vacates the orders filed on June 1, 2012 sustaining the demurrer as to the first, third and fourth causes of action and granting the motion to strike. Upon reconsideration of the demurrer and motion to strike, the demurrer is overruled as to the first and third causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action and the motion to strike is denied as to paragraph 16 and the prayer and off calendar as to paragraph 19. Assuming without deciding that the first and third causes of action do not relate back to the original complaint for statute of limitations purposes, nonetheless the complaint does not disclose on its face that the first and third claims are necessarily time-barred. Nor are those claims uncertain. The fourth cause of action fails because the tort of strict liability is inapplicable to the facts alleged, not is it likely that this deficiency can be cured by an amendment. The punitive damages allegations are sufficient because liberally construed the third claim alleges liability for promissory fraud for which punitive damages are available, not bad faith breach of contract as to which punitive damages are not available. In light of the ruling on the demurrer to the fourth cause of action, the motion to strike paragraph 19 is moot. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: 737 LA PLAYA HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION et al VS. LA PLAYA CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And M,Oiton To Compel Pltfs Form Interrogatory, Special Interrogatory And Request For Document Production Responses; Motion To Deem Matter Admitted Pertaining To Akitech'S Request For Admissions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 20, 2012, Line 20. CROSS DEFENDANT AKITECH ASSOCIATES, ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS AKITECH'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Form Interrogatory, Special Interrogatory And Request For Document Production Responses; Motion To Deem Matter Admitted Pertaining To Akitech'S Request For Admissions. Pro Tem Judge Trent Thornley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EAST WEST BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, AS VS. TED CARROLL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 20, 2012, Line 8, PLAINTIFF EAST WEST BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied in its entirety. Defendants' objections to paragraph 20 of the declaration of Ms. Shivers-Wynn are sustained. Because the records that purportedly provide the bases for plaintiff's calculation of its damages are neither attached nor specifically identified, the declarant's testimony about those records lacks foundation and violates the secondary evidence rule. This is particularly true as to asserted damages such as "escrow advances" that very likely did not originate with plaintiff or its predecessor. Once this objection is sustained, plaintiff has not met its burden of presenting evidence to support the required element of damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
